summary: A collaboration between the Quantum Science Center and Quantum Systems Accelerator—national quantum information science centers led by Oak Ridge and Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratories—enabled Fermilab and MIT Lincoln Laboratory to demonstrate cryoelectronic control of ion traps. This February 2026 proof-of-principle experiment used Fermilab-developed cryoelectronics, integrated with MIT Lincoln Laboratory’s ion-trap platform, to manipulate ions at cryogenic temperatures, reducing thermal noise and improving sensitivity. The approach could replace extensive room-temperature controls, crucial for scaling ion-trap quantum computers. Farah Fahim of Fermilab noted this could "accelerate the timeline for scaling quantum computers." MIT’s Robert McConnell emphasized remaining challenges but said the achievement "lays the foundation for hybrid-integrated systems." This marks a concrete advance toward scalable quantum computing.
